maestro-implement.current_description: Execute track tasks following TDD workflow. Single-agent by default, --team for parallel Agent Teams, Sub Agent Parallels. Use when ready to implement a planned track.

evals.query: ok the plan looks solid, let's start building out the auth_flow track now
I'm ready to implement -- kick off the dark_mode_20260301 track in team mode with parallel workers
resume implementation from where we left off yesterday on the api_refactor track, phase 2 task 3
let's start coding the tasks from that plan we just finished, single agent is fine
execute the payment_integration track -- we've got 3 phases, use --parallel to speed it up
time to implement, run the user_settings track with kraken and spark workers
the spec and plan are approved, go ahead and implement the notifications feature track
pick up the bug fix track and start working through the tasks
start implementing phase 2 of the db_migration track, phase 1 is already done
let's plan out how to implement a user authentication system for my Django app -- I need a spec and phased breakdown
can you create a new track for refactoring the database layer? I want to spec it out first
show me how far along we are on the current tracks -- what's done, what's left?
I need to undo the last phase of implementation on the auth track, there's a regression in the login flow
review the code changes from the api_refactor track against the spec and check for security issues
I want to set up maestro in this new React project so we can start using tracks
write a Jest unit test for the UserService.createUser method with mocked database calls
note down that we decided to use PostgreSQL instead of MySQL for the user store
help me design a real-time notification system -- this needs deep discovery, multiple components, websocket architecture
what's the status of the payment_integration track? how many tasks are left in phase 2?

maestro-note.current_description: Capture decisions, constraints, and context to persistent notepad. Priority notes are injected into every session and implementation run.

evals.query: remember that we decided to use Redis for caching instead of Memcached -- the ops team mandated it
make a priority note: all API responses must include X-Request-ID in headers per the compliance audit
save this as a note -- staging DB credentials rotate every 30 days, last rotated Feb 15
show me what's in the notepad right now, I want to see the priority context
clear the working memory section, most of those items are from last week and resolved
jot down: the legacy /v1/users endpoint is deprecated, redirect to /v2/users by March 15
prune the notepad -- some of those working memory items are from completed tracks
add to manual notes: always run migrations with --fake-initial on first deploy to staging
note this constraint from the product team: max file upload size is 10MB, videos not supported yet
add a JSDoc comment to this function explaining the retry logic and backoff strategy
create a README section about how to set up the development environment with Docker
write API documentation for the /users and /orders endpoints using OpenAPI spec
create a new track for implementing the notification system -- I want to spec and plan it
update the CLAUDE.md file with the new bun test command instead of npm test
what does the product.md context file say about our target users and key features?
I want to capture this workflow as a reusable skill for future projects
save the output of this API call to a local JSON file for reference
set up maestro for this project -- I need the product context and tech stack files

maestro-revert.current_description: Git-aware revert of track, phase, or individual task. Safely undoes implementation with plan state rollback.

evals.query: undo the implementation of the auth_flow_20260228 track -- it broke the login page for SSO users
roll back phase 2 of the current track, the database migration was wrong and corrupted the index
revert the last task I implemented on the api_refactor track, the test approach was fundamentally flawed
I need to undo everything from the dark_mode track and start the implementation over with a different approach
the payment integration track caused regressions in checkout, revert the whole thing
undo phase 3 of the notifications track so we can re-implement with the corrected spec
roll back the user_settings track -- the spec was incorrect, product changed requirements
revert task 'add-webhook-handler' from phase 1 of the integrations track
take back all changes from track db_migration_20260301, we need to use a different migration strategy
git revert HEAD~3 -- I want to undo the last 3 commits on this branch
undo my last edit to server.ts, I preferred the previous version of that function
reset the staging database to yesterday's backup, something went wrong with today's deploy
roll back the Docker deployment to the previous image tag v2.3.1
can you undo the changes to package.json? I don't want that new dependency after all
revert the PR #247 that was merged yesterday -- it introduced a memory leak
remove the feature flag for dark mode from the LaunchDarkly config
delete the test fixture files we created in the last session, they're not needed anymore
restore the .env.production file from the git history, someone accidentally overwrote it

maestro-review.current_description: Code review for a track against its spec and plan. Verifies implementation matches requirements, checks code quality and security.

evals.query: review the auth_flow track implementation -- does it match the spec we wrote?
check if the dark_mode track implementation covers all the acceptance criteria from the requirements
do a code review on the current in-progress track, I want to make sure it's solid before we merge
verify the payment_integration track -- check code quality, test coverage, and any security issues
I finished implementing the user_settings track, can you audit the code against the spec?
review what we've built for the api_refactor track, especially the breaking changes
run a review on the notifications track before I mark it complete -- check intent match and security
validate the db_migration track against the plan, did we miss any tasks?
review --current, let's see how the active track holds up against the spec
can you review this pull request on GitHub? here's the link: github.com/myorg/myrepo/pull/247
look at my submitOrder function and tell me if there are any edge cases I'm missing
review the architecture design doc I wrote for the new microservice -- is the approach sound?
I want to do a security audit of the entire codebase, not just one track
can you peer review this utility function I wrote? it handles date parsing with timezones
check the CI test results from the last GitHub Actions run and tell me what failed
review the changelog entries before we cut the v2.4.0 release
look at the git diff for the last 5 commits and summarize what changed
create a new track for the search feature -- I want to spec and plan it out

maestro-status.current_description: Show track progress overview with phase/task completion stats, next actions, and blockers.

evals.query: how are we doing on the tracks? give me an overview of everything
what's the progress on all the active development tracks right now?
show me where things stand with the current implementation -- phases, tasks, blockers
how many tracks are done, how many are still in progress?
what should I work on next? which track or task is up?
are there any blockers on the current tracks I should know about?
give me a status report on all maestro tracks -- completion stats and next actions
what's left to do on the active track? how many tasks remain in this phase?
where did we leave off last session? what's the next task I should pick up?
git status -- show me the working tree
what's the status of the CI/CD pipeline? did the deploy to staging go through?
show me the test results from the latest test run
how many open GitHub issues do we have in the backlog?
what's the state of the staging environment? is it healthy?
check if the build passed on the main branch
what's the PR review status for my open pull requests?
how many TODO comments are scattered across the codebase?
show me what's left in the current Jira sprint

maestro-setup.current_description: Scaffolds project context (product, tech stack, coding guidelines, product guidelines, workflow) and initializes track registry. Use for first-time project onboarding.

evals.query: I just cloned this repo and want to set up maestro so we can start tracking features
initialize maestro in this project -- product context, tech stack, guidelines, the works
set up the project context files for maestro -- this is a new Next.js e-commerce app
let's onboard this codebase to the maestro workflow, it's a Python FastAPI backend
run maestro setup with --reset, I want to redo the product definition and tech stack
scaffold the .maestro directory with all the context files -- product, tech stack, guidelines, workflow
this is a brand new project, let's get maestro initialized before we start any feature work
I want to use the maestro workflow for this repo -- where do I start? what do I need to set up?
configure maestro for this monorepo -- it has a React frontend in apps/web and a Go backend in services/api
set up the development environment for this project -- install dependencies, configure Docker, etc.
initialize a new git repository and set up the branch strategy
create a new Next.js project from scratch using create-next-app
configure ESLint, Prettier, and Husky hooks for the codebase
set up GitHub Actions CI/CD pipelines for test, build, and deploy
generate an AGENTS.md file that describes this codebase for AI agents
initialize the database schema and run the seed script
create a new feature track for adding user authentication
set up environment variables for local development -- .env.local with API keys

init.current_description: Generates AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md files using the WHAT/WHY/HOW framework. Explores the codebase and produces minimal (<100 line) context files with progressive disclosure.

evals.query: generate an AGENTS.md file for this repo so AI agents understand the project structure and conventions
create CLAUDE.md for this codebase -- I want Claude to know the build commands, tech stack, and rules
I need an AGENTS.md that covers what this project is, how to build it, and the coding conventions
rebuild the AGENTS.md from scratch -- reset, the codebase has changed significantly since it was generated
create context files so any AI coding agent understands this repo -- progressive disclosure, under 100 lines
generate CLAUDE.md with build commands, test commands, and project rules for this TypeScript monorepo
I want both AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md that describe this codebase accurately -- explore and generate them
update the CLAUDE.md to reflect the current state of the project -- we switched from npm to bun and added new packages
make an AGENTS.md with the WHAT/WHY/HOW framework for this Python data pipeline project
write a README.md for this project with installation instructions, usage examples, and contributing guide
create API documentation for all the REST endpoints using Swagger annotations
set up maestro with product context, tech stack, and guidelines for this project
write a CONTRIBUTING.md guide for open-source contributors
read the existing AGENTS.md and tell me what it says about the project
explain what AGENTS.md is and why I'd want one in my repo
create a new track for implementing the notification system
add a new section to the existing CLAUDE.md about our database migration process
generate a getting-started guide for new developers joining the team

maestro-new-track.current_description: Create a new feature/bug track with spec and implementation plan. Interactive interview generates requirements spec, then phased TDD plan. Use when starting work on a new feature, bug fix, or chore.

evals.query: I want to add dark mode support to the app -- let's create a track with spec and plan
there's a login timeout bug where sessions expire after 5 minutes instead of 30, let's plan the fix
new feature: implement real-time notifications using websockets and a Redis pub/sub backend
create a track to refactor the database connection pooling -- it's causing timeouts under load
I need to build an admin dashboard with user management, roles, and audit logs -- let's spec it out
start a new bug track for the broken file upload on mobile Safari -- files over 5MB fail silently
plan out the work for adding Stripe payment integration with subscriptions and invoicing
new chore: upgrade all major dependencies including React 19, Next.js 15, and TypeScript 5.5
I want to spec out an OAuth2 integration with Google and GitHub providers for our login page
I need to design a complex multi-tenant SaaS platform with per-tenant database isolation and custom domains
let's do deep discovery on the HIPAA compliance requirements -- this needs thorough domain analysis
start implementing the dark_mode track that we already planned
what's the status of the current tracks? how far along are we?
review the auth track implementation against the spec
undo phase 2 of the api_refactor track -- the migration was wrong
set up maestro for this project -- I need the product context and tech stack
help me brainstorm feature ideas for the next quarter's roadmap
write a product requirements document for the new dashboard feature

maestro-design.current_description: Deep discovery and specification for ambitious features. Full BMAD-inspired interview with classification, vision, journeys, domain analysis, and FR synthesis. Same output contract (spec.md + plan.md) as new-track but far richer. Use for multi-component systems, regulated domains, or unclear requirements.

evals.query: I need to design a multi-tenant SaaS platform with per-tenant data isolation, custom domains, and usage-based billing
let's do deep discovery on building a HIPAA-compliant patient records system with audit logging and role-based access
design an event-driven microservices architecture for our e-commerce platform -- order processing, inventory, payments, shipping

help me design a complex RBAC system with dynamic permissions, SSO via SAML/OIDC, and comprehensive audit logging
deep dive into requirements for a financial transaction processing system with reconciliation, compliance reporting, and fraud detection
I need full ceremony specification for a regulatory compliance dashboard -- SOC2, GDPR, data residency controls
design a multi-region deployment strategy with automatic failover, data replication, and conflict resolution
let's do thorough discovery for an AI-powered content moderation pipeline -- classification, appeals workflow, human review queue, policy engine
create a track for adding a dark mode toggle to the settings page -- pretty straightforward UI change
I need to fix a bug where the login form doesn't validate email addresses correctly
